title: Founder, Azimuth Labs
summary: Applied anthropology and AI product consultancy. Work centers on bringing ethnographic method to the design, evaluation, and governance of AI systems.

title: Anthropology and AI (edited volume)
summary: An edited collection gathering anthropological and social scientific perspectives on AI, its design, and its effects.

title: Public HTTP MCP server for a personal knowledge graph
summary: An anonymous-access Model Context Protocol server exposing a 563-node Schema.org knowledge graph of my research, teaching, and projects. Conforms to MCP 2025-06-18 Streamable HTTP, advertised via OpenAPI, agents.json, and an A2A agent-card.
approach: Treat a personal site as a knowledge object: graph-first, then compile renderings (JSON-LD, llms.txt, RDF Turtle, MCP) off the same data. Every agent surface is derived, not hand-authored.
reflectiveNote: I underestimated how long the claude.ai browser-side CORS handshake would take to get right. Session headers needed explicit exposure; the GET SSE listener needed a 200 empty stream handler. The mcp-adapter plugin gave me most of the transport for free but its stubs required careful, small patches.

title: AI Anthropology Toolkit
summary: Open-source Jupyter notebooks that let ethnographers apply modern NLP and ML to qualitative fieldwork data. Covers entity recognition, topic modeling, and document clustering, with Colab-first packaging.

title: Chair, AI and Anthropology Topical Interest Group
summary: Founding chair of the SfAA AI and Anthropology TIG. Built the charter, brand guide, editorial calendar, and report templates. Grew the group to 50+ members in its first year.

title: Advanced AI for Applied Anthropologists (workshop)
organization: Society for Applied Anthropology, annual meeting
summary: Full-day NSF-funded workshop co-facilitated with Bryce Macher. Walks practitioners through prompt design, eval frameworks, and tool selection grounded in applied research questions.

title: Journal of Business Anthropology, AI special edition (co-editor)
summary: Co-editing a special edition on AI and business anthropology with Adam Gamwell. Focus is on how anthropological methods inform real-world AI product and organizational decisions.

title: Human Organization, AI special edition (co-editor)
organization: Human Organization / Society for Applied Anthropology
summary: Co-editing an AI special edition with Lora Koycheva under lead editor Lenore Manderson.

title: Anthro to UX (podcast host)
summary: Long-running interview podcast on careers at the intersection of anthropology and user experience. Guests include practitioners, hiring managers, and scholars.

title: Anthropology in Business (podcast host)
summary: Interview podcast on how anthropologists work inside companies, from research to strategy to product.

title: Cross-Cultural Consumer Insights, Fordham MKBU 3459
summary: Teaching support and curriculum contribution for a cross-cultural consumer insights course. Designed interview guide feedback protocol and best practices document for five student groups.

title: UX and Design Research, early career
summary: Multiple in-house and consulting roles in UX research, design anthropology, and consumer insight. Groundwork for later applied-AI product work: the craft of fielding, coding, and translating qualitative evidence into decisions.
reflectiveNote: The career shape only made sense in retrospect. At the time it felt like drift between method-heavy and product-heavy roles. Now it reads as deliberate: each role added one capability that the AI product work required.
